title: Fermented Crispy Thai Naan Bread Flatbread
description: A Thai-inspired flatbread recipe that combines the crunch of crispy naan bread with the tanginess of fermented flavors. This recipe is perfect for a quick snack or meal that's both kid-friendly and raw-friendly.
instructions: Combine yeast, warm water, and sugar in a bowl and let it sit for 5 minutes, until frothy.
Add 2 cups of all-purpose flour, salt, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it ferment in a warm, draft-free place for 1-2 hours, until doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Shape each ball into a thin circle, about 6-7 inches in diameter.
Heat a non-stick skillet or grill over medium-high heat, cook the naan bread for 1-2 minutes per side, until crispy and golden brown.
immediately submerge the cooked naan bread in an ice bath to stop the cooking process and help maintain crispiness.
Repeat with the remaining dough pieces,
